frames: The agent is initially in a low, crouched position, then begins to extend its body and legs upwards.
It continues to rise, achieving a tall, upright posture with its body nearly vertical and legs bent.
The agent loses balance and begins to fall backward, its body tilting rapidly towards the ground.
It lands flat on its back, with its body horizontal and all four limbs bent and lifted off the ground.
The agent remains supine on the ground, with its limbs slightly adjusting their bent positions.
It continues to lie motionless on its back, with its limbs held in a static, bent posture.
The agent shows very subtle, minor movements in its bent limbs while still lying flat.
The agent remains completely on its back, with its limbs static and bent, showing no further attempts to move.
